item.title: Fender Player II Modified Active Jazz Bass®, Maple Fingerboard, 3 Tone Sunburst (D3) (56025) — $929
item.description:
$929 · Excellent · Franklin Guitar Works

Weight - 9lb 8oz

Up for sale is a Fender Player II Modified Active Jazz Bass Maple Fingerboard, 3 Tone Sunburst finish. This one came to us in great shape and plays fantastic. The electronics have been play tested and are working as they should. This one may have some very light surface scratching, but it is in overall excellent condition. This one comes with Fender DLX gig bag.

Gear up and get ready to take your performance to the next level with Player II Modified. Featuring key upgrades for the modern guitarist and bassist, these versatile instruments are designed to handle anything you can throw at them.

The Player II Modified Active Jazz Bass® is a classic bass with the modern player in mind. Everything about the neck is designed for fast and fluid playability, from the Modern “C”-profile with silky satin urethane finish on the back to the comfy 9.5”-radius slab rosewood or 1-piece maple fingerboard with smooth rolled edges and 20 medium jumbo frets. The alder body provides classic Fender punch and is available in a variety of exciting new finishes. The Player II noiseless™ pickups deliver classic Jazz Bass® growl while eliminating unwanted noise while the all-new active preamp with passive tone control and EQ section gives players the perfect tool to dial in their favorite tones. The 4-saddle HiMass™ bridge, tapered-shaft tuners and TUSQ® nut all combine to keep tuning incredibly stable for even the most demanding performances.

Features
Modern "C" Neck Profile with 9.5“-Radius Fingerboard and Rolled Edges
New Noiseless™ Jazz Bass® Pickups
New Active Preamp with Passive Tone Control and 3-Band EQ
4-Saddle HiMass™ Bridge
Tapered-Shaft Tuning Machines
TUSQ® Nut


SKU:0147522300

item.title: Cool Vintage 60s MIJ Phantom Violin Bass in Cherry Sunburst — $390
item.description:
$390 · Good · Steve's Gear Garage

Amazing lightweight 'Beatle" bass from Phantom Guitar Works. Nice playing and remarkable condition for a nearly 60 year old instrument. Neck plays well, good intonation. Has some cosmetic imperfections, but don't we all at this age? Finish still shows beautifully and has a great woody tone, rings well even unplugged. Intonation is good. Only notable issues are it's in need of fresh strings, and the tone knob rotates continuously. Plugged in you definitely get that Beatle vibe, and the skull and crossbones on the headstock give you instant pirate credibility. Please write if you need more info or would like to see other pix. It does include the rough original chipboard case.

item.title: Fender Japan FSR Collection Hybrid II Jazz Bass 2025 - Daphne Blue Matching Headstock — $1,799
item.description:
$1,799 · Excellent · Japanese Guitar Company

Beautiful Fender FSR Collection Hybrid II Jazz Bass finished in Daphne Blue with a matching painted headstock, maple fingerboard, and white pearloid pickguard.

Made in Japan in 2025, this is a special FSR Collection model that combines classic Jazz Bass styling with a number of modern, player-friendly features. The Daphne Blue finish looks fantastic against the maple fingerboard, while the matching headstock and pearloid pickguard give this bass a distinctive look that stands apart from a standard Hybrid II.

The maple neck features a satin urethane finish on the back for a smooth, comfortable playing feel, along with a gloss urethane finish on the front of the headstock. The 9.5" fingerboard radius offers a slightly flatter and more modern feel than a vintage-style Fender radius while retaining the familiar feel of a traditional Jazz Bass.

Just cleaned and professionally set up here at Japanese Guitar Company with low action. It is currently playing great with a comfortable feel up and down the neck.

Overall, the bass is in excellent condition with only very light signs of previous use. There are some shallow dents and light buckle-rash impressions on the back of the body. They are difficult to capture in photographs and are not especially noticeable under normal viewing, but they are present and should be noted. Aside from that area, the bass remains exceptionally clean with very little visible wear.

The frets show virtually no wear, and the electronics have been tested and are functioning properly.

Includes the original Fender padded gig bag, paperwork, adjustment tools, and accessories shown.

Case: Soft

Scale Length: 34“

Nut Width: 1.515“

Fret Wear: 9.6/10

Action at 12F (4th): .08“

Action at 12F (1st): .07“

Weight: 9lbs 7.5oz

Overall Condition: 9/10

Cool Factor: 11 out of 10

item.title: Fender Vintera III Early '70s Jazz Bass, Rosewood Fingerboard, Black (Q1) (29529) — $1,209
item.description:
$1,209 · Excellent · Franklin Guitar Works

Weight - 9lb 6oz

Up for sale is a Fender Vintera III Early '70s Jazz Bass Rosewood Fingerboard Black finish. This one came to us in great shape and plays fantastic. The electronics have been play tested and are working as they should. This one may have some very light surface scratching, but it is in overall excellent condition. Case not included.

The Fender® Vintera III series delivers meticulously crafted vintage recreations that capture the authentic look, feel, and tone of iconic Fender guitars from the '50s, '60s, and '70s. The Vintera® III Early '70s Jazz Bass® features an alder body and maple neck with maple or rosewood fingerboard for classic Fender tone that's full of punch and clarity. The early '70s "C"-shape maple neck sports binding and block inlays with 7.25" radius maple or rosewood fingerboard and vintage-tall frets.

At its heart, you'll find a pair of vintage-style early '70s single-coil pickups that deliver all the punchy and articulate, warm and woody tone that made Fender® famous. Other features include a vintage-style 4-saddle bridge for rock-solid intonation and sustain and vintage-style tuning machines for enhanced tuning stability.

Embrace the authentic vintage spirit and legendary sound of Vintera® III – where every detail tells the story.

Features

  • Alder Body

  • Maple neck with 7.25" Radius Round-Laminated Rosewood or 1-Piece Maple Fingerboard and Vintage Tall Frets

  • Early '70s "C"-Shaped Neck with Bound Fingerboard and Block Inlays

  • Vintage-Style Early '70s Pickups

  • Fender Stamped Vintage-Style Non-Reverse Open-Gear Tuning Machines

  • 4-Saddle Vintage-Style Bridge with Slotted Steel Saddles

  • Era-Correct Details

item.title: Fender American Vintage II 1960 Precision Bass, Rosewood Fingerboard, Olympic White (U9) (50702) — $2,009
item.description:
$2,009 · Excellent · Franklin Guitar Works

Weight 8lbs 15oz

Up for sale is a Fender American Vintage II 1960 Precision Bass. This one has a few small neck dings up by the 1st and 2nd fret positions. They are on the back of the neck, a few have been sanded, filled and refinished but there are still a couple small ones that you can feel. There is also a very small chip on the back upper horn that was filled and repaired as pictured. Its still in great shape and it plays great and sound fantastic. The electronics have been play tested and are working as they should. This one may have some very light surface scratching, but it is in overall excellent condition. This one comes with a case.

Built with period-accurate bodies, necks and hardware, premium finishes and meticulously voiced, year-specific pickups, each instrument captures the essence of authentic Fender craftsmanship and tone.

The late '50s to the early '60s was a watershed period in the development of the Precision Bass®, a time of transition that would see the introduction of many new appointments that would become synonymous with the P-Bass®. By 1960, the design of the P-bass had eclipsed the earlier cues culled from the Telecaster® - now the P-bass had a Strat®-style headstock shape, comfort-contours, pickguard mounted electronics and 4-saddle bridge. Adorned in 3-Color Sunburst and topped with a 4-ply tortoiseshell pickguard, the 1960 Precision Bass represented a more refined, enduring aesthetic for the model.

Featuring a premium alder body, Pure Vintage '60 Split-Coil P-Bass pickup and a substantial "C" shape hard rock maple neck mated with a 7.25" radius slab rosewood fingerboard, the American Vintage II 1960 Precision Bass reproduces the quintessential tone and feel of the original.

The instruments in the American Vintage II series are direct descendants of the original Fenders: designed for players with a fine appreciation for vintage Fender tone and feel and built with unmatched quality, down to the last screw. These are Fender electrics in their purest form: Fender American Vintage II, the stuff of legends.

Features:
Alder Body
Gloss Nitrocellulose Lacquer Finish
Pure Vintage '60 Split-Coil Precision Bass® Pickups
"C"-Shaped Neck Profile
Slab Rosewood Fingerboard
Pure Vintage Reverse Open-Gear Tuners

item.description:
$679 · Excellent · Franklin Guitar Works

Up for sale is a Fender Player II Mustang Bass PJ, Rosewood Fingerboard, Hialeah Yellow finish. This one came to us in great shape and plays fantastic. Step into the spotlight and experience iconic Fender sound and style with the Player II Mustang Bass PJ a stage-ready bass guitar with contemporary updates to power your performance and inspire your playing.

The Player II Mustang Bass PJ radiates timeless Fender charm, but under the hood, it's primed for today's players. Everything about the neck is designed for fast and fluid playability, from the Modern “C”-profile with silky satin urethane finish on the back to the comfy 9.5”-radius slab rosewood or maple fingerboard with smooth rolled edges and 20 medium jumbo frets.

A classic alder body is available in both timeless Fender finishes and never-before-seen colors unearthed from the archives. Player Series Alnico V Split-Coil P bass and Single-Coil J bass pickups offer sweet highs, growling mids and seismic lows that elevate any genre. The 3-way toggle switch lets you easily dial in everything this versatile pickup set has to offer, while an adjustable 4-saddle bridge, single-groove steel saddles and open-gear tuners ensure precise tuning stability for the flexibility to explore endless sonic possibilities.

Features
Alder Body
Modern "C" Neck Profile
9.5“-Radius Maple or Rosewood Fingerboard with Rolled Edges
Player Series Alnico V Split-Coil Precision Bass and Alnico V Single-Coil Jazz Bass Pickups
4-Saddle Bridge with Single-Groove Steel “Barrel” Saddles
Open-Gear Tuning Machines

item.title: 2025 Spector Ish X Spector 15th Anniversary Holoflash Euro Limited Edition 5 String — $2,799.99
item.description:
$2,799.99 · Excellent · Ish Guitars

2025 Spector Ish X Spector 15th Anniversary Holoflash Euro Limited Edition 5 String

Weight: 9.3 lbs 

DESCRIPTION

The Spector Holoflash finish is back, baby! For the 15th anniversary of Ish Guitars, Spector agreed to my crazy idea to bring Holoflash back from the dead. Originally debuted in the late 80's, I always thought Holoflash was the coolest - it was still produced on Asian-made Legend models when I started Ish Guitars at age 16, and I've always wanted to have it on a higher-end Spector. What better time to make Holoflash happen than for our 15th anniversary?

The Euro series has never had a proper production run of Holoflash basses. For the first time ever, we have 15 of them: five 4 string, five 5 string, and five 6 string. Each bass is numbered 1 through 15 and will include a special Ish Anniversary Certificate commemorating this special run.

Thank you to Spector for making this happen, and thank you to everyone who has supported my little business over the years and have helped turn Ish into the industry leader it is today. Its because of you that I’m able to help bring these incredible bass creations to life!

SPECS

Cosmetic Condition: Excellent condition with some very light scratches on the back.

Weight: 9.3 lbs 

Case: Gig Bag 

Year: 2025

Body: Solid Maple (chambered for weight relief)

Fingerboard: Ebony 

Scale Length: 35" 

Inlays: Laminated Abalone 

Nut: Brass 

Hardware: Black 

Tuners: Gotoh GB350 lightweight 

Pickups: EMG  

Preamp: EMG BTS 

SETUP & INSPECTED BY OUR TECH TEAM

All of our guitars come with a FREE professional setup to a comfortable action as specified by that instrument’s manufacturer. Our setups always include inspection of (and necessary adjustment to): action, intonation, tremolo angle, truss rod, frets, nut, bridge, and more. We can accommodate special setup requests - additional charges and/or shipping delays of 1-2 business days could occur.

INDIVIDUALLY PHOTOGRAPHED

After 15 years of innovation in our photography process, Ish Guitars has developed a way to get you the most accurate true-to-life color of every instrument we sell. We never embellish, oversaturate, or misrepresent our images, so that your buying experience can be as much like visiting our showroom as possible. Keep in mind that various computer screens can show colors a bit differently, so the color on your screen may not match the real thing perfectly no matter how hard we try. Also, unless otherwise noted, we individually list and photo every piece of gear that comes through our doors.

HEAR A LIVE DEMO

Experience our innovative Video Appointment service! Connect with our Gear Experts to hear this instrument played live over Zoom and receive one-on-one guidance. Contact us to schedule your appointment!

item.title: Fender Jazz Bass 1964 — $10,499.99
item.description:
$10,499.99 · Very Good · Acme Guitars

Here’s a 1964 Fender Jazz bass. It has been setup and sounding and playing great like pre-CBS Jazz bass should. This is one of the nicer playing and sounding pre-CBS Jazz basses that has come through the shop in awhile. Early on in its life it had a sunburst finish and was covered over with a white nitro finish. This bass has been played and has had long time owner who stated that when he bought it was white. It appears to have its original finish underneath the white finish. A bit of the white nitro is on the back of the bridge pickup cover. Pots have the date code 304-6420. Dating it to 20th week of 1964. Pickups are original and sound wonderful. Nitro pickguard has shrunk a bit but is still fully functional. Frets have some play wear but still has a decent amount of original height to them. Bass weighs in at 9.25 lbs. Comes with a Gator hardshell case. See pictures for condition.

item.title: Fender Japan JB-62/LH ’62 Reissue Left-Handed Jazz Bass 95-96 - 3-Tone Sunburst — $1,299
item.description:
$1,299 · Very Good · Japanese Guitar Company

Very cool and increasingly hard-to-find left-handed Fender Japan JB-62/LH Jazz Bass finished in classic 3-Tone Sunburst.

Made in Japan during Fender Japan’s highly regarded Fujigen era, this one carries serial number U008758, dating it approximately to 1995–1996. Left-handed Fender Japan basses were produced in considerably smaller numbers than their right-handed counterparts, making clean examples increasingly difficult to find.

The JB-62/LH recreates the classic early-’60s Jazz Bass design with a comfortable vintage-style neck, 7.25" fingerboard radius, rosewood fingerboard, vintage-style frets, stacked control knobs, tortoiseshell pickguard, and traditional Jazz Bass pickup configuration.

Just cleaned and restrung. The bass is playing nicely and is ready to go.

Overall, it is in very good condition, especially considering its age. There are some light scratches, small marks, and normal signs of use throughout, including a few small impressions and surface marks on the front of the body. Nothing is especially distracting, and the rich 3-Tone Sunburst finish still presents beautifully.

The frets show only light wear with plenty of life remaining. The neck is comfortable, the electronics have been tested, and everything is functioning as it should.

The colored string-end retainers visible behind the bridge appear to be aftermarket additions. They do not affect the function or playability of the bass and could likely be removed if desired.

Includes the padded gig bag shown.

Case: Soft

Scale Length: 34“

Nut Width: 1.5“

Fret Wear: 9/10

Action at 12F (1st): .07“

Overall Condition: 8.3/10

Cool Factor: 11 out of 10

Google AI says:

Neck

  • Scale Length: 34 inches.
  • Fretboard Radius: 7.25 inches.
  • Frets: 20 vintage-style frets.
  • Headstock: Large CBS-era headstock with a bold black Fender logo and bullet truss rod adjustment.

Hardware & Electronics

  • Pickups: Original split-coil humbucking pickup, known for producing a punchy, warm, and dark tone.
  • Controls: 1x Volume, 1x Tone.
  • Tuners: Open-gear "Fender" stamped tuners.
  • Bridge: Standard top-loading Fender bridge with threaded or grooved barrel saddles.
  • Pickguard: Usually 3-ply black, white, or faux-tortoiseshell.

item.title: 1963 Gibson ES-330 TD - Sunburst - OHSC — $11,899.03
item.description:
$11,899.03 · Excellent · Andy Baxter Bass & Guitars Ltd

1963 Gibson ES-330 TD in excellent condition. Sunburst finish, mahogany neck with a rosewood fretboard, block position inlays and nickel hardware. The nut has been replaced - otherwise all original, including the Lifton case. (The original nut is included) 

The ES-330 shares the body shape of the ES-335, ES-345 and ES-355 and is a lightweight, lively-sounding guitar when played acoustically or plugged in. It's fitted with powerful P-90 single-coil pickups, which are nicely balanced. There are a few very minor dings and scuffs to the body and neck. There's a little more wear around the edges of the headstock, but everything remains structurally sound with no breaks or repairs. There is some playwear to the fretboard. The frets are in excellent condition. The G string tuner is slightly bent, but it functions correctly.

This guitar has been set up in our workshop with great action and a fresh set of D'Addario 10-gauge strings. The pots are all correct with unbroken solder. The pot dates that are visible are dated early 63. The pickups are nicely balanced with a lovely warm tone from the neck pickup and a crisp bite from the bridge. Weight: 6 lbs 4.5 oz / 2.84 kg. Serial: 109661. Nut width: 43 mm. Depth at 1st fret: 21.25 mm.

Franklin Guitar Works - Who We Are
FGW is a division of MIRC, LLC (Musical Instrument Reclamation Company), located in La Vergne, TN.  MIRC is the world’s largest refurbisher of stringed instruments.  We partner with the leading manufacturers in the industry, helping them recondition their distressed instruments. All of our instruments have been inspected, set up, and refurbished by our certified technicians to ensure you are receiving a quality instrument.

Hours of Operation
Our online Business hours are Monday - Friday 7:30 am to 4:00 pm CST. Closed on weekends and holidays.

Shipping Policy
Free ground shipping within the continental USA. Items under $20 will have a $9.95 shipping charge. We do not offer international shipping.
We cannot ship to a PO Box. Orders require a physical mailing address to be processed.
All orders over $500 will be shipped with a direct Signature Requirement to ensure it is received. If the signature requirement is refused, the customer will assume responsibility for the package once it reaches the delivery address.

Orders are processed Monday through Friday, 7:30am - 4:00pm CST. Shipments are prepared in the order that they are received. We make every effort to expedite orders as quickly as possible, but we cannot guarantee ship dates. Please allow up to 3 business days for tracking information. 

Thank you for trusting us with your business.

75th Anniversary Precision Bass Collection

Introducing the 75th Anniversary Precision Bass® Collection: Celebrating three-quarters of a century of foundational tone and revolutionary design. Each 75th Anniversary Precision Bass model showcases the rich heritage and legendary tones that have made the P Bass® an unrivaled icon. From vintage purists to progressive players seeking cutting-edge features, rest assured there's a model that will deliver. Immerse yourself in the legacy of the Precision Bass and own a piece of Fender® history with the 75th Anniversary Precision Bass Collection.

American Vintage II 1951 Precision Bass®

Celebrate the birth of the electric bass with the 75th Anniversary American Vintage II 1951 Precision Bass. Crafted with a resonant swamp ash body finished in nitrocellulose lacquer, this instrument radiates vintage charm. The 1-piece maple neck and 7.25" radius fingerboard with vintage frets provide the smooth playability that players have cherished for decades. The 75th Anniversary 1951 Single-Coil Precision Bass® pickup delivers authentic tones with that foundational thump and clarity that has defined the sound of popular music for decades. Other appointments include a vintage-accurate serialized bridge with Pure Vintage Precision Bass® Fiber Saddles, Vintage-Style Reverse Gear Bass tuners and a 1-Ply Black Phenolic pickguard. This limited-edition model captures the essence of the original Precision Bass, a must-have for collectors and bass enthusiasts.

This is a Momose MJ-EWC/AC SP18, a rare left-handed model that was specially built as a semi-custom order for the Deviser 2018 Dealer Convention Limited Edition.

Its most striking feature is the beautifully figured Exotic Burl Maple top, finished in an elegant natural-to-blue burst that showcases the wood's unique character. The headstock features a matching Burl Maple veneer with the same finish, creating a sophisticated matching-headstock design.

item.title: Fender Musicmaster Bass 1978 - Olympic White — $1,499.99
item.description:
$1,499.99 · Good · Ernie Williamson Music

At our Springfield MO location. Some normal wear here and there for a 70's guitar. Structurally solid and plays great. Does not come with a case.

The 1978 Fender Musicmaster Bass in Olympic White is a vintage, short-scale (30'') student bass. It features a solid poplar or ash body, a maple neck with a rosewood fretboard, and a single grey-bottom pickup.

Key Features and Specifications

  • Scale Length: 30'' (short-scale), making it very comfortable and easy to play for smaller hands.
  • Body: Originally made of poplar, but by 1978, Fender often used ash. Olympic White is an opaque color that tends to yellow or "cream" over time due to UV light exposure.
  • Electronics: Features a single coil pickup (famously borrowed from six-string guitar parts) with one master volume and one master tone control.
  • Bridge and Tuners: Has a top-loading hardtail bridge with just two saddles (like early Telecaster basses) and small, cloverleaf-style tuning machines.

item.title: Fender Precision Bass 1958 - the cleanest See-Thru Ash Body Blonde Gold'gard P Bass in the world ! — $64,995
item.description:
$64,995 · Excellent · The Guitar Broker

This is the ultimate all original very early production Custom Colored Pre-CBS Precision Bass w/a Maple neck w/all early 1958 features in untouched original condition, it has its original See-Thru White/Blonde finish over a very lightweight Ash body dated 9/58 in its pickup cavity & weighing in at a scant 7 lbs. 14 oz; Its super clean maple neck is dated 5/58, these final issue Fender P's w/maple necks are ridiculously rare w/their super wide & full sized feel, it's sporting a very low serial # (30XXX) stamped on its 4-bolt neck plate, its original Gold anodized pickgard brings this all together, its split Pickup is super smooth w/that booming rock & funk tone !, its pot date codes are from the 25th week of 1958, its so cool from its nickel tuners to its threaded saddle bridge, its all original in all respects & still retains its bridge & pickup covers along with every single screw from new, this is perhaps Leo's prettiest P Bass & has a gorgeous patina for a 68 year old original, it plays & feels incredible, other than having a few dings & some buckle rash on the back, she's still Exceptional in all respects, perhaps the cleanest original & most desirable examples on Earth, she comes complete in her original Tweed hard case.

item.title: Spector Legend 4 Classic Walnut Burl & Ash — $1,000
item.description:
$1,000 · Excellent · Kentucky Music

This bass is in great shape. The original owner kept it in a climate-controlled smoke free environment. There are some really light scratches on the burl top. We feel like most of it could be easily buffed out. It weighs 8lb 5oz which is pretty light compared to most basses. This model is extremely hard to find. We see only one listed on Reverb and it is twice what we are asking. However, ours does not come with a case. Please message us with any questions.

From the web:

The Legend 4 Classic’s exquisitely carved Ned Steinberger–designed body, beautifully rendered in walnut burl on lightweight ash, yields style and performance to meet the needs of demanding players everywhere — professionals to weekenders. The Legend 4 Classic’s easy-playing 5-bolt neck is built from three pieces of hard maple and fortified with a 2-way heavy-duty truss rod for immaculate stability and precision; on top, a pau ferro fingerboard with dot inlays delivers classic Spector playability without the price premium. Onboard you’ll find a pair of custom-wound Bartolini P/J pickups and a proprietary TonePump Jr. active preamp packing 12dB of cut and boost where it counts.

Electronics

Bartolini P/J pickups

The Spector Legend 4 Classic comes loaded with a set of custom-wound Bartolini pickups. Built exclusively for Spector, these hum-canceling pickups deliver an open, resonant sound and unmatched note articulation. This classic P/J pairing features single-conductor shielded cable that minimizes unwanted interference. The wide range of tones that these pickups exhibit is well-suited for any playing style.

TonePump Jr. active preamp

The Legend 4 Classic comes equipped with a proprietary preamp circuit derived from the much-discussed Petr Michalík TonePump. Every TonePump Jr. is built using good-sense engineering and is ear tested, by Petr himself, for sound and context. Highly interactive bass and treble shaping amounting to 12dB equips the Legend 4 Classic for just about any style of music you’re likely to encounter.

Tonewoods Lightweight ash body

Lightweight, balanced ash serves as the foundation for many classic guitars and basses. So it’s no secret why Spector chose ash as the body wood for the Spector Legend 4 Classic, which is acoustically resonant, tonally complex, and hangs comfortably off the shoulder.

3-piece bolt-on maple neck

A neck built from three pieces of hard maple, secured to the body using five screws and strengthened with a rugged dual-action truss rod, gives the Legend 4 Classic incredible stability between tune-ups and a cutting tone to match.

Spector Legend 4 Classic Electric Bass Features:

  • NS style and sonics at an incredible value
  • Priced to serve players everywhere — pros to weekenders
  • Built from lightweight ash and capped with an eye-catching walnut burl top
  • Stable 3-piece bolt-on maple neck with dual-action truss rod
  • Pau ferro fingerboard with classic dot inlays
  • Loaded with custom-wound Bartolini P/J pickups
  • TonePump Jr. active preamp circuit delivers 12dB of interactive bass and treble shaping

item.title: Dingwall SP-1 4-String Bass - Gloss Black Metallic — $2,510
item.description:
$2,510 · Brand New · The Guitar Sanctuary

Vintage Passive Soul Meets Modern Multi-Scale Precision

The Guitar Sanctuary is thrilled to present the Dingwall SP-1 4-String Bass, stunningly appointed in a Gloss Black Metallic finish with a striking Matte Cocoa Gold pickguard. For players who demand the timeless, organic punch of a classic passive P-bass but refuse to compromise on pitch-perfect intonation and modern playability, the SP-1 (Super-P) stands as the ultimate evolution of the world's most recorded electric bass.

Hand-finished in Canada, the SP-1 features a highly resonant two-piece Alder body paired with a fast, satin-finished 5-piece Maple neck. Driven by a single, high-output Dingwall FD3-P Neodymium pickup and a traditional passive Volume/Tone layout, this bass delivers a smooth, muscular, and exceptionally clean signal path with zero active noise. It is pure, unadulterated plug-and-play authority that sits perfectly in any studio or live mix.

Sanctuary Expert Tone Profile: Punchy, woody, and extraordinarily balanced. The passive Neodymium pickup offers incredible touch sensitivity—delivering all the warmth and mid-range "growl" of a vintage '60s P-bass, but with an open, high-fidelity clarity that never gets muddy.

Why Multi-Scale Wins on a 4-String

While multi-scale designs are famous on 5-string instruments, the benefits on a standard 4-string are revolutionary for feel, string tension, and tonal consistency:

  • Optimized String Tension: With scale lengths graduating from 32” on the G-string to 34.25” on the E-string, each string operates at its optimal physical tension. The E-string is remarkably tight and focused, while the G-string retains a sweet, singing warmth without feeling stiff.
  • Consistent Hand Attack: Because string tension is balanced evenly across the entire fretboard, you can maintain a uniform right-hand picking or plucking technique without compensating for loose or tight strings.
  • Flawless Intonation: The fanned-fret layout guarantees perfect, sweet-tempered intonation from the open strings all the way up to the highest frets.
Key Performance Attributes & Specifications
  • Best For: Studio Session Work, Rock, R&B, Motown, Blues, and Worship.
  • Compared To: Custom Shop vintage P-Basses, high-end boutique passive basses.
  • Body Construction: 2-Piece Alder Body with Matte Cocoa Gold Pickguard.
  • Neck Profile: 5-Piece Maple Neck with a comfortable Medium-Thin "C" shape.
  • Fingerboard: Maple with 9.45" Radius and 18% hard nickel silver banjo-sized frets.
  • Scale Length Range: Multi-Scale 32” (G) to 34.25” (E).
  • Electronics: 100% Passive. Master Volume and Master Tone.
  • Pickups: 1 x Dingwall FD3-P Neodymium Pickup (high output, smooth yet aggressive).
  • Hardware: Custom lightweight Hipshot/Dingwall tuning machines and surface-mounted adjustable bridge.
  • Case: Premium Deluxe Dingwall Gig Bag included.

Stock photos shown, individual color and grain will vary from bass to bass.

item.title: Dingwall SP-1 5-String Bass - Candy Green — $2,605
item.description:
$2,605 · Brand New · The Guitar Sanctuary

Classic Passive Vibe Meets Uncompromised Multi-Scale Low End

The Guitar Sanctuary is proud to showcase the Dingwall SP-1 5-String Bass, stunningly finished in an eye-catching Candy Green Gloss paired with a premium Matte Cocoa Gold pickguard. Engineered for players who crave the earthy, legendary punch of a traditional passive P-bass but demand the modern pitch precision and low-end definition of a tight, clear B-string, the SP-1 (Super-P) is the absolute peak of 5-string passive bass design.

Proudly hand-finished at the Dingwall shop in Canada, this masterfully crafted instrument features a resonant two-piece Alder body and a fast, satin-finished 5-piece Maple neck. Equipped with a custom-wound, high-output Dingwall FD3-P Neodymium split-coil pickup and a traditional passive Volume/Tone circuit, this bass provides an ultra-clean, low-noise signal path. It is a pure, plug-and-play studio and stage weapon that sits perfectly in any mix without the need for active EQ tweaking.

Sanctuary Expert Tone Profile: Warm, punchy, and highly articulate. The passive Neodymium split-coil pickup captures the classic, woody mid-range "honk" of a vintage '60s P-bass but expands on it with remarkable touch sensitivity, modern clarity, and an exceptionally tight, thunderous B-string.

Why the Multi-Scale Super-P Outperforms Traditional 5-Strings

Traditional 5-string P-basses are plagued by uneven string tension. Dingwall's fanned-fret multi-scale system perfectly balances the physics of each individual string:

  • A Defined, Tight B-String: Spanning a full 35" scale on the low B, the string remains incredibly taut, snappy, and clear—eliminating the loose, "floppy" B-string common on traditional 34" scale basses.
  • Warm, Singing Treble Strings: With the G-string set at a comfortable 32" scale, string tension is sweet and slinky. It retains its natural warmth and bending ease without sounding stiff or thin.
  • Even Hand Attack: Balanced tension across the maple fingerboard allows you to keep a uniform right-hand plucking technique across all five strings without having to alter your attack.
Key Performance Attributes & Specifications
  • Best For: Session Work, Classic Rock, R&B, Motown, Blues, and Worship.
  • Compared To: Vintage Fender Custom Shop P-Basses, boutique custom passive 5-strings.
  • Body Construction: Resonant 2-Piece Alder Body with Matte Cocoa Gold Pickguard.
  • Neck: 5-Piece Maple Neck with a comfortable Medium-Thin "C" shape profile.
  • Fingerboard: Premium Maple with a 9.45" Radius and 18% hard nickel silver banjo-sized frets.
  • Scale Length Range: Multi-Scale 32” (G) to 35” (B).
  • Electronics: 100% Passive. Master Volume and Master Tone.
  • Pickups: 1 x Dingwall FD3-P Neodymium Split-Coil Pickup.
  • Hardware: Lightweight Open-Back Hipshot/Dingwall tuning machines and adjustable bridge with custom riser.
  • Case: Premium Deluxe Dingwall Gig Bag included.

item.title: Ibanez SRFF806-BKS Multi-Scale w/ AGUILAR PREAMP UPGRADE OBP-3TK/PP - fanned fret — $850
item.description:
$850 · Good · Luke in Nashville

Upgraded Aguilar OBP-3TK/PP preamp, Bartolini pickups, and multiscale (fanned fret) design give you serious punch on the low end and clear supple highs. Obvious wear to the finish on the front, a small divot on the edge of the fingerboard on the treble side near the nut, a few other small dings, and small plastic plugs where the stock preamp had a couple switches- all should be in the photos. Here's the copy/paste info about the bass and the Aguilar preamp:

The Ibanez SRFF806 is a quality instrument designed for the experienced, seasoned musician. Its ash body is attached to a 5-piece jatoba/bubinga SRFF6 neck with a rosewood fretboard, medium frets and custom fretted inlays. The Mono-Rail V bridge is the perfect match for a multi-scale instrument, allowing for independent positioning of each string's mini-bridge section to providing excellent intonation and superior string vibration transference. Includes Bartolini BH1-5 pickups for a full-range, warm, punchy tone.

The Aguilar OBP-3 is a three-band boost and cut bass, mid, and treble preamp, that has high headroom, low distortion, and the ability to reproduce the full dynamic range of your instrument. The OBP-3 is Aguilar's most flexible onboard preamp design, providing +/-18dB of cut and boost at 40Hz, +/- 16dB of cut and boost at either 400Hz or 800Hz and +/- 16dB of treble cut and boost at 6.5 kHz. Includes separate Treble, Mid, and Bass pots. Mid pot is push/pull for Midrange frequency selection.

item.description:

The Ibanez Jet King II JTKB200 Electric Bass Guitar. Rare find. Sounds and plays great! This is so cool! This was part of the short lived Jet King II series; made from from Nov 2003 to Oct 2004. The shapes and styling were conceived as a nod to the 60s Ibanez offerings Retro cool, modern flare, one that doesn't come available often. It's an Ibanez Jet King JTKB200 from 2003. It's in terrific condition with flat wounds strings and setup, ready to go. (But you may want to adjust to your preference/playing style).smooth and buttery. Ships fast, so get it fast!!

High-quality, right-handed electric bass with a solid mahogany body and a maple neck. It features a 4 string configuration and comes with a gig bag for easy transportation and storage. This model is part of the Ibanez Jet King series, known for its excellent craftsmanship and sound quality. Whether you are a beginner or a seasoned musician, this bass electric guitar is a great choice for any music enthusiast. Setup and ready to play with med-low action, strung with beautiful Fender 9050 flatwound strings, feels excellent! (If you prefer round wounds in can change strings) great condition with only a few minor light scuffs and scratches, metal discoloration. Pictures are of the actual item you will recieve. Ships fast so get it fast FedEx home delivery.

Model: Ibanez Jet King Bass JTKB200

Finish: brown sunburst (BS)

Body: mahogany

Neck: maple with rosewood board, 12" radius, 34" scale

Mods: none

Pickups: Ibanez soapbar style single coil x2

Case: Gig bag

Ibanez JTKB200 Jet King bass:

Although the current trend for new bass guitars seems to be one of active electronics, sleek body shapes with overextended horns and an abundance of strings, there is still a healthy market for long established and classic designs from the past.

Ibanez is certainly one of the leading manufacturers of these production line hi-tech, hi-spec machines, but with the Jet King the company has taken a step or two back to offer a more modest and retro approach.

Here we see a return to their early days of, er, interesting shapes, passive electronics and an eye for the unusual.

The Jet King is not so far removed from those early Gibson clones that got the company into trouble, and is fully packed with classic motifs that definitely draw on those chunky basses from the past.

Ibanez has already shown that it can produce great instruments when re-inventing these classic models (such as the excellent Iceman bass from the nineties), but this bass version of its JTK4 electric guitar reveals a less flamboyant appearance and a feel that actually sends you right back to the seventies.

It's a middle-ground approach that features minimalist cutaways to the slab body with a simple forearm chamfer to provide a little more comfort, a bound fretboard and a deep two-tone sunburst finish that serves to fully nail this retro look.

Nothing is left to chance here as all the individual features make their own bold visual statements - check out the two scratchplates, the huge bridge plate and the use of chrome covers over the single-coil pickups.

However, the three controls sit neatly on the lower scratchplate and the jack socket is positioned on the body edge for a cleaner look and lower risk of damage when the instrument is plugged in.

All this helps to give the bass a very distinctive personality that can be felt as well as seen, as there are practical elements in the design here too.

For instance, the bridge may have a large base plate but the active parts are relatively standard and very robustly made.

The individual saddles are kept neatly locked in line by a pair of hefty retaining pins that promise to withstand the attacking forces from even the most rigorous of pick players.m. There's good string spacing though, so lots of room to pull, pluck or slap as desired.

The neck is also generous in width and is well profiled to offer a positive grip and a feel that is much more Gibson than Fender. The fretboard extends beyond the neck joint and is squared off for a sharper look and to accommodate that top fret.

The upper scratchplate has no real practical purpose, as bass players rarely strum, but it does present further visual originality. However, that central screw looks more like an afterthought than a well-conceived placement.

The headstock is lightly back-tilted, so negates the use of a string tree, and thanks to its relatively small size and sealed gear tuners the instrument pleasingly provides the best playing experience possible from something with such a compact upper body horn. Very well done indeed.

With an eye on this generally vintage design one would naturally assume a similar approach in the sound, and indeed the Jet King fully delivers this - with style.

Individual volume controls are the ideal choice here and the blending of these will provide the majority of the tonal variations available. The tone control itself sets the mood with a bias towards the bottom-end or the cleaner overtones but there are no extremes here, just a tight range of viable sounds.

The pickups may look like mini-humbuckers but they are actually very capable single-coil units.

With one positioned centrally to the body and the other set towards the bridge the expectation is for a harder edge to the sound, with slightly less dynamic hollows, and this is pretty much the case.

Think of a good hard thud but with vastly improved note clarity and you'll be in the ballpark. This positioning also tonal value of the Jet King.

It's a pleasing sound too as it excels with warm and fruity characteristics and responds well to the naturally limited range provided by the single tone control and passive circuitry.

While the bridge pickup is mostly there to support this central unit it actually adds a great deal to the quality of the sound variations. It allows the bass to grunt and growl with the best of them, but with a very distinctive slant.

In fact the qualities of the overall sound are very much as individual as the presentation. While it undoubtedly has some tonal characteristics from the past, it manages to serve them up with a little more spice for a subtle variation that will provide appeal to a much broader taste.

Without doubt Ibanez has some very impressive bass endorsees on its books, yet the majority favour the company's more modern designs.

However, for the rest of us, passive basses remain popular - there is an appeal to the full-on (if less dynamic) delivery of these old workhorses.

With that in mind this bass is ideally suited to the modern day root-note player where a big fat sound is all that really counts. It looks like a pretty standard instrument yet the Jet King bass is full of surprises.

With a unique voice, practical features and pseudo classic looks it offers a worthy addition to the immensely popular twin passive pickup range of basses. It has an air With a unique voice, practical features and pseudo classic looks it offers a worthy addition to the immensely popular twin passive pickup range of basses. It has an air of authority that holds the eye and offers a playing experience that many bassists will find both attractive and comfortable.

It's certainly a tough little cookie, too, and that's what makes it great to take out on the road. So if you want something unusual but with a vintage sound and appearance, this is a very unique bass at a very reasonable price.

item.description:

Glarry GP P Style electric bass guitar in excellent used condition. Fully functional and structurally sound, showing only the lightest signs of handling or play wear, with no significant scratches, dings, or cosmetic blemishes.


Designed with accessibility in mind, this bass features an ultra lightweight contoured body paired with a comfortable C shape maple neck and a smooth fingerboard. It comes equipped with a single classic split coil pickup, managed by straightforward master volume and master tone controls, delivering a punchy, familiar low end thump that works across any musical style.


An incredibly lightweight, easy playing instrument in near pristine shape, making it an excellent choice for beginners, a comfortable practice bass, or a reliable, budget friendly backup.

item.description:

4-String Acoustic Bass The Dragonfly 4 Port acoustic-electric bass blends sound and style in a visually and sonically striking instrument that?s perfect for stage performances, studio sessions, or jam sessions. The body itself is entirely constructed from Macassar Ebony in Michael Kelly?s unique Port-style construction with a full 34" scale length for a warm tone and plenty of body. The C-shaped neck profile makes for comfortable playing, and the Abalone and Pearl dragonfly inlay in the fretboard gives this bass a distinct, eye-catching visual flair. Unique Port Construction Michael Kelly?s guitars are built to deliver the best possible sound using unique bracing and construction for new sound port placements on the instrument. An offset front port creates an unbroken connection between the bridge and the neck that resonates more and provides clearer and louder sound. A side port also acts as a personal monitor, projecting sound upwards so you can hear your playing more clearly. Excellent Electronics The built-in Fishman Presys + Bass? system lets you plug in and play anywhere. On top of the Bass, Mid, and Treble controls, the pickup features everything you?d need for playing and performing, including a tuner, a notch control for fighting feedback, a phase switch, and additional tone-shaping in the form of a Brilliance control. Plug in, dial in your sound, and play anywhere. This custom blend of electronics makes the Pinnacle a versatile instrument for any bass player. item.title: Dingwall Combustion Nashville Edition 5-String Bass - Whalepool Blue - Maple Fretboard — $3,845
item.description:
$3,845 · Brand New · The Guitar Sanctuary

Boutique Multi-Scale Performance Meets Legendary Neve Electronics

The Guitar Sanctuary is proud to showcase the Dingwall Combustion Nashville Edition 5-String Bass, stunningly finished in Whalepool Blue over a figured Quilt Maple Top. By merging North American premium materials with precision manufacturing, Dingwall’s Combustion Series delivers elite boutique playability and massive, studio-grade tones at an incredibly accessible price point. Each instrument receives its final, rigorous setup and inspection at the Dingwall headquarters in Canada, ensuring flawless action right out of the gig bag.

The Nashville Edition stands apart with its premium solid Swamp Ash body, a lightweight 5-piece maple neck, and an advanced electronic package. Equipped with three custom-voiced Dingwall FD-3N Neodymium pickups, this bass is paired with an active Rupert Neve Designs 2-Band Preamp. The result is an ultra-low-noise signal path with immense headroom, punchy mids, and sparkling highs—perfect for clean studio tracking or driving a heavy stage mix.

Sanctuary Expert Tone Profile: Clear, punchy, and incredibly articulate. The Rupert Neve preamp provides a pristine, high-fidelity foundation that lets the natural, woody voice of the swamp ash and quilt maple top shine through with massive low-end authority. Why the Fanned-Fret Multi-Scale System Wins

Dingwall is the pioneer of the fanned-fret design. By optimizing the scale length of each individual string, this multi-scale system solves the age-old issues of traditional 5-string basses:

  • The Definitive B-String: At a full 37" scale, the B-string is incredibly tight, distinct, and clear—no more muddy low notes.
  • Consistent Tension & Tone: With the G-string set at a comfortable 34" scale, it retains its natural warmth and tension. This even tension across the fretboard allows for a consistent hand attack from string to string.
  • Improved Intonation: Individual string scaling ensures perfect intonation across the entire maple fingerboard.
Key Structural Specifications
  • Best For: Modern Gospel, Progressive Metal, R&B, Session Work, and Live Performance.
  • Compared To: Custom Canadian Dingwall builds, high-end active multi-scale basses.
  • Body Construction: 2-3 Piece Swamp Ash with a figured Quilt Maple Top and Matte Black pickguard.
  • Neck Profile: Fast, medium-thin C-shape 5-piece maple neck with a 9.45" radius maple fingerboard.
  • Scale Lengths: B: 37″, E: 36.25″, A: 35.5″, D: 34.75″, G: 34″
  • Pickups: 3 x Dingwall FD-3N Neodymium high-output pickups.
  • Electronics: Rupert Neve Designs 2-Band Preamp with Master Volume (Push/Pull Active/Passive), Pickup Blend, Master Tone, Bass, Treble, and a 3-way toggle (Middle Pickup Solo, Middle + Blend, Blend Only).
  • Hardware: Smoked Chrome Hipshot/Dingwall lightweight tuning machines and custom adjustable bridge with riser.
  • Case: Deluxe Dingwall Gig Bag included.

Photos are of the actual bass for sale, SN 19347


Made in: China / Canada (Hybrid Manufacturing)
Body: Swamp Ash, with optional Quilt Maple top
Neck: 5-piece Maple, Bolt-On
Fingerboard: Maple, 24 'banjo-sized' frets, 9.45-inch radius
Scale Length: 5-String: 34" to 37"
Pickups: Dingwall FD3n, Neodymium Magnets
Preamp: Rupert Neve Designs 2-Band EQ
Controls: Master volume w/ push-pull active/passive switch, bridge/neck pickup blend, master tone, bass, treble, 3-way toggle (middle pickup solo, middle + blend, blend only)
Bridge: Dingwall Minimalist Bridge *(the first run of Nashville Edition Combustions will have individual string bridge units in smoked chrome.)
String Spacing at the Bridge: 18mm

Blend Options
Toggle switch UP: middle pickup solo (blend bypass)
Toggle switch MIDDLE, blend knob turned counter-clockwise: bridge humbucker
Toggle switch MIDDLE, blend knob centered: all three pickups
Toggle switch MIDDLE, blend knob turned clockwise: neck humbucker
Toggle switch DOWN: blend only

Tuners: Licensed Hipshot Ultralite
Nut: Graphtech Black Tusq
Case: Dingwall Padded Gigbag

item.title: Guild Jumbo Junior Bass 2026 - Natural — $752.25

The Guild Jumbo Junior Mahogany gives you a jumbo-like body shape in a smaller size. It makes an ideal travel guitar, yet it sounds good enough to be played anywhere. The guitar features a solid Sitka spruce top combined with mahogany sides and an arched mahogany back—resulting in a full and well-balanced sound with lots of richness and sustain. The Guild Jumbo Junior Mahogany guitar also sports a pau ferro fingerboard and bridge, a real bone nut and saddle, a mother-of-pearl rosette, and a beautiful Natural satin finish. An integrated Fishman-designed GT-1 pickup system with Volume and Tone controls faithfully reproduces the guitar’s acoustic sound when it is amplified.

Back Arched Flamed Maple
